name: Italian Fish Soup
recipeIngredient: 3 Leeks, cleaned and sliced (white part only)
6 cups Fish stock, clam nectar or water
1 cup Dry white wine (such as Soave)
1 lb White fish, boneless fillet cut into small pieces (cod, seabass, monkfish)
2 lbs Combined, of any or all of the following:
Clams, soaked in fresh water for 1 hour then drained
recipeInstructions:
Serves 8. Chopped parsley for garnish.
1. Prepare and clean the seafood as desired (clam shells on, crab and shrimp cleaned).
2. In a large pot, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add leeks, onion, celery, and garlic. Cook until onions are transparent.
3. Stir in mushrooms and cook for 2-3 minutes more.
4. Pour in fish stock, clam nectar or water, tomato sauce, and white wine. Bring to a gentle simmer.
5. Taste and add cayenne pepper and salt as needed.
6. Add the seafood, starting with shellfish that takes longer to cook (clams).
7. Simmer until all seafood is cooked through, about 10-15 minutes for clams and mussels, 3-4 minutes for shrimp and crab.
8. Serve in large bowls over spaghetti. For a richer meal, add an additional serving of fish per person.
